mnemonics.title: Linear Combination Definition
mnemonics.content: COIN: Coefficient × Original Vector, Add all, Number of terms - Remember C-O-I-N = Coefficients + Original vectors + Into + Numbers

mnemonics.title: Span Meaning
mnemonics.content: SPAN = S-earch P-lace A-ll N-umbers - All possible combinations of given vectors

mnemonics.title: Linear Dependence
mnemonics.content: DEPEND = DEpendent if Non-trivial combination Depicts zero - Non-trivial combo = zero vector means dependent

mnemonics.title: Independent Check
mnemonics.content: INDEPENDENT = If No Possible combination Exists that Makes Non-zero vector Disappear - only trivial gives zero

acronyms.usage: Only trivial combo produces zero vector
Used to determine linear dependence and find combinations
Linearly independent spanning set for a vector space

visualTips.visualization: Think of two arrows from origin. Their span fills entire plane - like stretching and adding rubber bands in both directions to cover all points
Imagine a single elastic string stretched from origin - only points along that line can be reached
Two parallel arrows pointing same or opposite direction - one is just stretched version of other
Two arrows pointing in different directions forming a V - need both to cover the plane

analogies.analogy: Like mixing paints - adding different amounts (scalars) of primary colors (vectors) to get any color in the spectrum (span)
Like a restaurant menu - all possible meals you can order from a set of ingredients
Having three team members where one is just the sum of others - you don't really need all three
Having unique skills - each person contributes something no one else can provide
Like having the minimum number of building blocks to construct everything in a 3D space
